title: 10 S&P 500 Stocks Set Up for a Rebound After Recent Selloff
author: David Wagner
description: The S&P 500 fell to its lowest level since August 2025 before bouncing 1.15% on Monday following positive comments from President Trump about Iran talks. However, Iranian officials denied any talks occurred, creating market uncertainty. Despite ongoing geopolitical tensions, investors believe the worst may be over. The article identifies 10 S&P 500 stocks that have dropped 15-37% in recent weeks and now appear undervalued with strong analyst upside potential of 22-80%.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock has fallen 15-37% over four weeks but appears undervalued by 21-62% with analyst upside of 22-80%. Company shows steady organic growth of 2-3%, strong cash generation, and market share gains despite mixed industrial environment.

Listed as a rebound candidate with significant recent decline but strong growth momentum. International expansion and new running category products provide growth catalysts with analyst upside potential.

Despite reported sales decline, maintains positive organic growth of ~3% with improving profitability. Stock reflects transition phase with recovery potential driven by nutrition and biosciences business.

title: 10 War-Beaten Stocks Rally Monday On Trump Truce Talk—Despite Iran Denial
author: Piero Cingari
description: Markets rallied sharply on Monday following President Trump's announcement of a five-day halt to U.S. military strikes on Iranian energy infrastructure and claims of productive peace talks, despite Iran's swift denial of any negotiations. The S&P 500 gained 1.64%, with stocks hardest hit by the Middle East conflict—particularly cruise operators, airlines, and homebuilders—experiencing the strongest rebounds. Gold miners and construction-related ETFs also performed well amid the relief rally.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Cruise operator rallied 7.71% on Monday as one of the stocks hardest hit by the conflict, benefiting from Trump's truce announcement and relief from airspace closures and fuel cost surges.
Cruise operator surged 8.39% on Monday, rebounding sharply after falling 23% since the start of the month due to Middle East airspace closures.
Cruise operator gained 7.37% on Monday as part of the relief rally for war-impacted stocks following Trump's peace talk announcement.
Building materials company rose 5.55% (3.92% by 9:40 a.m. ET) as homebuilding and construction sectors rebounded, having fallen 22.12% month-to-date.
Airline stock gained 5.25% on Monday as airlines benefited from the truce announcement, rebounding from a 15.38% month-to-date decline.
Airline rallied 4.57% on Monday, recovering from a 19.65% month-to-date decline due to Middle East conflict impacts.
Homebuilder gained 4.45% on Monday as the homebuilding sector rebounded, having declined 17% month-to-date.
Chemical company rose 5.07% on Monday as part of the broad relief rally, recovering from a 21.13% month-to-date decline.
Gold mining company gained 4.71% on Monday as gold miners were among the best performers, rebounding from a 26.16% month-to-date decline.
Logistics company rose 3.29% on Monday as part of the relief rally, recovering from a 17.33% month-to-date decline.
Auto manufacturer gained 3.81% on Monday as part of the broad market relief rally.

Homebuilding ETF gained 4.21% on Monday as construction-related stocks rebounded from war-related declines.

title: Industrial Coatings Market Size to Surpass USD 176.06 Billion by 2035
author: Towards Chemical And Materials
description: The global industrial coatings market, valued at USD 117.13 billion in 2025, is projected to grow to USD 176.06 billion by 2035 at a CAGR of 4.16%. Asia Pacific dominates with 40.19% market share. The market is driven by sustainability targets, technological innovation, and a shift from solvent-based to low-emission technologies including water-borne, powder, and UV-curable systems. Key trends include electric vehicle integration, smart coatings with self-healing properties, and circular chemistry adoption.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Announced merger of equals with AkzoNobel in November 2025, positioning the combined entity as a premier global coatings company with broader portfolios and improved competitive dynamics.
Major player in the growing industrial coatings market benefiting from increased demand for protective coatings, infrastructure investment, and shift toward sustainable solutions.
Key market participant positioned to benefit from strong demand in North America driven by infrastructure refurbishment, energy transition projects, and adoption of high-performance coatings.
Listed as a key company in the industrial coatings sector, benefits from market growth driven by sustainability trends and technological innovation.
Listed as a key company in the industrial coatings market. Benefits from growing demand for sustainable coating solutions and technological innovation in the sector.
Key market player announced an all-stock merger with Axalta in November 2025, creating one of the world's largest coatings entities with enhanced growth prospects and profitability.

title: Construction Glass Market to Exceed US$ 190.3 Billion by 2030 Driven by Energy-Efficient Building Design, Rapid Infrastructure Development, and Advanced Glazing Technologies | Astute Analytica
author: Astute Analytica
description: The global construction glass market is projected to grow from US$ 110.9 billion in 2022 to US$ 190.3 billion by 2030, driven by energy-efficient building design, infrastructure development, and advanced glazing technologies. Key innovations include insulated glass units (IGUs), tempered glass, and laminated glass. Asia Pacific leads the market, with facades and curtain walls dominating applications. The float process remains the primary manufacturing method.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Identified as a top market player in the construction glass sector, which is experiencing significant growth due to rising infrastructure development and energy-efficient building design trends.
Major player in expanding construction glass market driven by global emphasis on sustainability, energy efficiency, and technological advancements in glass products.
Listed as a key market player in a growing industry benefiting from technological advancements and increased demand for energy-efficient construction materials.

title: [Latest] Global Automotive Adhesives and Sealants Market Size/Share Worth USD 13.18 Billion by 2035 at a 6.9% CAGR: Custom Market Insights (Analysis, Outlook, Leaders, Report, Trends, Forecast, Growth Rate, Value, SWOT Analysis)
author: Custom Market Insights
description: The global automotive adhesives and sealants market is projected to grow from USD 7.26 billion in 2025 to USD 13.18 billion by 2035, at a CAGR of 6.9%. Growth is driven by increased vehicle production, demand for lightweight materials, electric vehicle adoption, and stricter fuel efficiency regulations. Polyurethane-based adhesives dominate the market, with North America leading and Asia Pacific showing the fastest growth.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Identified as a major key market player in the growing automotive adhesives and sealants sector, benefiting from market expansion driven by EV adoption and lightweight material trends.
Key market player positioned to benefit from market growth driven by increased automotive production and demand for advanced bonding solutions.
Major player in the expanding automotive adhesives market, benefiting from innovation in polymer chemistries and growing demand for high-performance bonding solutions.
Listed as a key market player in a growing sector driven by vehicle electrification, lightweight material adoption, and stricter environmental regulations.
